paragraphs.body: You are watching this painter sitting in a vibrant green, crisp, tree ridden plot of grass, hammocks hanging from tree to tree, the sound of the wind through the trees and leaves all around you, while the sun is shining after the clouds covered it for most of the day. As you take a closer look, you see this beautiful, canvas with paint caked on it, a forgotten paint brush to its right. The paint brush has this right red handle where the paint it is chipping off, the type that gets stuck under your nails and clings to your skin. The bristles are fraying slightly, sticking out straight and to the sides, coated in this bright blue paint. Like the sky on a summer day, pure sky blue, looks almost edible. The paint glistens in the sun, looking as if it contains sparkles, the reflecting from the sun. The wind is blowing the smell of acrylic paint through the air, smelling like your elementary school’s art classroom, after a particularly messy painting project. The canvas in front of the painter is beautifully created, a vibrant scene of a fountain painted on it, hearing the sounds of a brush hitting the canvas, seeing the colors blend together like a stunning sunset painting the sky. The paint tubes are falling over each other, becoming jumbled together from the wind. Dirt and leaves are being blown into the blue coated paintbrush, creating the same feeling as when your hair gets stuck in your lip-gloss on a stormy or windy day.

annotations.context_left: gets stuck under your nails and clings to your skin. The bristles are fraying slightly, sticking out

paragraphs.body: On a particularly gloomy weekend day I was going on a walk, passing the huge statue of a random dinosaur. Looking around, seeing quite the characters out in about, being slightly nervous while passing. In front of me in this patch of grass surrounded by trees I see a painter sitting in this grass. What a beautiful sight in the middle of a gloomy day. I start to wonder what they are painting, trying to stare a little harder, almost burning a hole into the canvas. It truly brings a smile to my face when I see what it is about. My day instantly brightens, I wonder what their inspiration was behind it, why they chose this odd location, where they got their paint from, if they are from Pittsburgh or just passing by, all of these thoughts race through my mind as I pass by them continuing my walk to the botanical gardens. Really brings out the beauty people can create out of nothing. Bringing me back to a certain time in my childhood when something tragic happened, my dad stopped paying for the house my mom lived in, so we lost the house and life we had in an instant.

Losing the house is a staple in my childhood, a pivotal point in my life where I gained this newfound respect, love, and admiration for my mother. We had this beautiful large house in this lovely neighborhood that after my parents divorced, we were forced to move out of. Immediately, we were on the hunt for a new place to live. We stumbled upon this slightly weird one bedroom apartment that just happened to be districted for my elementary school. Walking into this apartment opening the door you were met with this skinny stairway, beige-yellow walls, light tan carpet, and a straight daunting view up to the apartment. Walking up the stairs each footstep sounding heavier then the last we walked up in fear of what would be on the other side. When we finally made it to the top there was not much to look at, the kitchen was teeny tiny, to the point two people could not stand in it at once, no pantry, the dining room not big enough for a table, living room just fit a couch. When walking past the kitchen we were met with what would be our bedroom, a plain, boring, sad box like room, immediately it felt like the walls were closing in on us. The room almost too small to fit a queen size bed in it, no nightstands in sight. The door to the bedroom opens directly to the door of the bathroom, this even smaller room where you could basically touch your elbows to each wall. All we could laugh and say its at least ours.

annotations.comment: excellent narrative! Again, your details are great :) You're writing has really evolved! It's amazing :)
annotations.context_left: light tan carpet, and a straight daunting view up to the apartment. Walking up the stairs each

paragraphs.body: Now, I know that apartment does not sound ideal, cramped beyond belief, sharing a bed and room with your mom all while navigating fifth grade. But what my mom did to it, oh my it was truly life changing. She brough life and beauty into it. The smell of cookies baking freshly in the kitchen still runs through my mind, the laughs shared between us when I spilled slime all on the carpet. The bellowing of laugher and forever memory of her spit that landed on my hand when we were brushing our teeth together in the bathroom. The meals that she created, the movie marathons we went through, my dog running crazy through the small walls of the place, the stories she told me, that I will hopefully tell again one day. The Christmas tree we had to squeeze into the corner, fairy lights we messily hung up on a random evening, the long nights of challenging fifth grade homework done at the table. My mom painted this dire situation into such a fond memory of mine, which is such a privilege to have. Something so tragic and traumatic of losing a house turned into amazing memories and loving times with my mom as we navigated through life together.

annotations.comment: Beautiful paragraph. The description is lovely and memorable :)
annotations.context_left: the carpet. The bellowing of laugher and forever memory of her spit that landed on my hand

paragraphs.body: Beauty is such a hard thing to capture. In my opinion, beauty is not about looks, it is about your outlook on life, never taking anything for granted, making the best out of every situation, remembering how amazing of a privilege it is to live every day, to never sway from who you truly are and what you believe in, to smile on tough days, to make other smile any day, to love ones around you. That is what beauty is to me, not some superficial stigma, but a true understanding and optimistic outlook of that around you . Sometimes situations, life, people, places turn out to be like a dirty used paintbrush. Where you feel stuck in place, like you are not able to move anywhere, to grow, to breathe. But you have to remember that these situations will have a beautiful outcome, even if it is not what you originally expected to happen, even if you have to convince yourself that it is okay. Everything happens for a reason. How you handle said situations, what beauty you create out of anything you are put through. Try your best every day to leave hints of your spirit everywhere, do not leave a person, place, surface, idea, untouched by you. Be like a used paintbrush, that makes something beautiful. That has the ability to be cleaned off and used again in creation. That can brighten someone’s day up just being with it for a few hours, that can work with others, that has the durability to withstand hard things, that leaves their mark. That all in all, is able to grow, change, help, create, love, all without losing its original self, even if the bristles change a little over time.

annotations.comment: This is a great start to a definition paragraph!
annotations.context_right: situation, remembering how amazing of a privilege it is to live every day, to never sway from

annotations.comment: This sentence, however, changes from definition to metaphor. It's still lovely, though!
Great revision! Your concrete and specific details are amazing. Your writing has really evolved this semester! It's so awesome to see. :)
